title: How I Met My "Boyfriend" (ft. @Sultan Sketches)
text:
A few years ago, I used to go on youth club retreats every December. Most of the people who went there were as young as me, while a small percentage of people were young adults ages 21 and above. They were mostly in charge of taking care of the minors. Everyone in the club was super nice, And I made quite the handful of friends there, and I still
talk to them to this day! This was my third year going on this retreat And usually, I'd be hyped up for it but (record scratch) Yes, there's a but. Sometimes it gotta be this way Unfortunately, I was going through a massive breakup And when I mean massive, I meant this breakup literally emotionally, mentally, and psychologically scarred me *rock music and flames continue* *squeak* Yeah, you get the picture *Awkward giggle* I don't want to push this breakup story too
much into your faces Since this story isn't going to be about that, but I just
want to emphasize that Breakups suck, they suck. It freaking sucks. Well, with my breakup, at least. I don't want to give myself excuses for the poor and
ridiculous decisions I made in this story. I just want to clarify one thing: I wasn't myself during this phase in my life. To me, this retreat was a saving grace since, 
at the very least, this would help me take my mind off things,
get myself active and busy. There were a lot of hands-on activities in
this retreat which involved: Team sports, open group conversations and reflection. Basically, a weekend filled with feel-good chemicals
for my "I just don't want to live anymore :)" brain I remember thinking to myself in the middle of a group discussion "Wow, I'm gonna leave this retreat without thinking about
who I'm trying not to think about" - "Hey Alex, how's that boyfriend of yours?" *dramatic music* Why are we still here *intense breath in* just to suffer *another intense breath in* every night? I can feel my... *wanting to cry but trying reallllllyyy hard not to* Okay, now this is important. If you were to find a friend going through a breakup you
either have two initial reactions, not including apathy. Number one: You console them You make an effort to be there for them and listen
to whatever they have to say Basically a support system Just want to say kudos to

title: How I Met My "Boyfriend" (ft. @Sultan Sketches)
text:
through a breakup you
either have two initial reactions, not including apathy. Number one: You console them You make an effort to be there for them and listen
to whatever they have to say Basically a support system Just want to say kudos to the real ones out
there supporting your homies Mmm, good friendship Or, on the other hand you may have this second reaction: [DON'T BE THIS PERSON]
"Oh, heyyy, they're single" And if you're this type of person, well, I got news for you You better bACK THE FRICK UP, YOU HORMONAL, RAGING ANIMAL! What are you doing!? At this point, it was too late to pull myself back together Because in the eyes of everyone I've exposed my vulnerability I didn't know much of the guy back then He was one of the oldest members in the club and was
already a member before I even joined I wouldn't call the two of us friends despite being
in the same club together Since we've never had any real one-to-one
conversation before To me he was just one of those guys who existed in the
club and his name was Steven... That same night, everyone was sitting around the
campfire playing Kiss, Marry, Kill And if you don't know this game basically
you're given three people To choose who you would want to kiss, who you would
want to marry, and who you would want to kill Simple as that To give the game a little more spice, we went by the order marry, kill, kiss Because, let's face it no one is about to get married
or killed soon in this game. The kiss, on the other hand, either held meaning or none at all Because, at the end of the day, no one is going to take
this game seriously because it's just a game [INSERT FORESHADOWING]
Because, at the end of the day, no one is going to take
this game seriously because it's just a game Everyone had their turn to go at it A lot of us were screaming and laughing as people were
breaking trusts and friendships It helped me loosen up a few knots in my stomach And, for the first time, I felt like I was truly there I was present. I was happy. Suddenly, my name got called up as one of the options for Steven. "Hmm, I would marry Jeanne, kill Maria, and kiss... ...Alex" - "Oooooh, spicy" "But, kind of illegal. But, whatever, you're turning 18 soon, right Alex?" - "Uh, yeah, next year." - "Ah darn, guess you'll have to wait a while for this one Steven"
- "You know, I should have killed you instead of Maria" - "Anyway Alex, choose from Angelo, Nathan, or Steven
to marry, kill, or kiss. Go!" Pause! Right here on

title: How I Met My "Boyfriend" (ft. @Sultan Sketches)
text:
next year." - "Ah darn, guess you'll have to wait a while for this one Steven"
- "You know, I should have killed you instead of Maria" - "Anyway Alex, choose from Angelo, Nathan, or Steven
to marry, kill, or kiss. Go!" Pause! Right here on my stupid, naïve, dumb face! At this point in time, this is what was going through my brain... "Wait... What the f-" - "Haha hu haha It's so bad that it hurts" *Crying in the corner* - "I don't understand It's not fair Life isn't fair. I just don't want to live anymore" - "Oh my god! He likes me! What the frig, He likes me!
I can just tell. - "Can someone tell Phillip to pass the crackers over here?" - "Everyone, shut up and no, Alex, He doesn't like you. Remember, this is just a game. Now our peer pressure levels are almost to boiling point
since everybody's attention is on us As we all know, Alex is a people pleaser who would
rather hurt herself than the people around her I suggest that we give the favor back to not
hurt Steven's feelings, if he has any" - "Wait what?! But, that would give him the wrong idea What if he actually like likes Alex? Wouldn't it be better to choose another candidate?" *big sigh* - "Listen Alex, the last thing that Alex would want us to
crush someone's feelings Secondly, why would Steven like us in the first place? We've never had a conversation with the guy before This whole thing is just a game You might as well believe 15 people are plotting to kill
Tyrone behind his back right now No one's going to take our answer seriously" - "Yes, yes, that's true."
- "You're right" - "That's true."
- "In a way, that makes sense." - "It's just a game." - "Fine then, since I'm the only one who has common sense here However, we shouldn't forget our goal:
To fix Alex's happiness" - "Well, what's your answer Alex?" uhh.. uuuhhhh - "Well, I-I guess uh, Marry Nathan, kill Angelo, kiss... Steven" *crickets* - "Ooooooooooo!" - "Remember guys, it's just it's just a game, you know,
no feelings attached It's not like I like him or anything" It's just a game, it's just a game.. Right?
